scope: Wire AgenC's external MCP client tool bridge through permission arbitration, approval prompts, MCP approval templates, and request-permissions RPC handling.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow upstream source root path

requiredBehaviors: Run external MCP client tool calls through AgenC's permission evaluator before client.callTool.
Block denied MCP client tool calls without dispatching to the external MCP server.
Propagate evaluator-updated arguments into the eventual client.callTool request.
Wire the session-owned MCP manager to provide evaluator, approval, and active-turn context to bridges before startup.
edgeCases: Standalone bridges preserve existing direct dispatch behavior when permission options are omitted.
Permission evaluator exceptions become tool errors instead of dispatching.
Reconnect-created bridges receive the same permission options as the original bridge.

requiredBehaviors: Render configured MCP approval templates before falling back to evaluator messages.
Build approval context payloads as MCP tool calls with server name, tool name, and raw arguments.
Pass evaluator-updated arguments to the approval prompt and later dispatch.
edgeCases: Missing approval template data falls back to the evaluator message.
Denied approvals return tool errors without external dispatch.
Template rendering works from actual MCP descriptor and bridge namespace data.

requiredBehaviors: Intercept external MCP request_permissions tools when a local request-permissions RPC is configured.
Normalize request arguments through the request-permissions RPC substrate.
Route approvals through the guardian request-permissions request shape.
Return approved or empty grants as JSON tool content without calling the external MCP server.
edgeCases: Invalid request_permissions arguments return a tool error.
Missing prompt wiring resolves to empty turn-scoped grants.
Approval failures resolve pending RPC state before returning an error.
